_meta.description: City-specific zoning and development standards for SB 1123 analysis
_meta.usage: Keyed by city name (matching Redfin CITY field). LA City is the baseline — other cities override where they differ.
_meta.data_gaps: See per-city 'unconfirmed' arrays for values that need verification from municipal code

Los Angeles.notes: All app defaults (proforma.js, listings_build.py) assume LA City rules

zone_mapping._note: Pasadena uses RS (single-family) and RM (multi-family) with density-per-acre suffixes

height_ft._note: RS/RM-12 height per Ordinance 7371 (Feb 2021) Table 2-3. Varies by lot width, not zone.

height_notes.RS_all: 28 ft on lots <75 ft wide; 32 ft on lots >=75 ft wide. All RS zones identical. 2-story max — no 3-story.
height_notes.RS_constraint_summary: 3-story effectively prohibited: no 3rd-story plate height defined in Table 2-3, 28-32 ft ridge cap, and 2nd story limited to 50% of 1st story floor area
height_notes.RS_measurement: Height measured from lowest existing grade at exterior wall to highest ridgeline (§17.40.060)
height_notes.RM-32: 32 ft on lots <60 ft wide; rear 40% up to 36 ft on lots >=60 ft
height_notes.hillside_overlay: 28 ft max at any point if HD/HL overlay applies; 35 ft from lowest grade
height_notes.hl1_overlay: HL-1 overlay limits rear structures to 23 ft plate / 32 ft ridge on 60 ft+ lots

plate_heights._note: Per Ordinance 7371, Table 2-3 (§17.22.040). Plate = top of wall framing, not ridge.
plate_heights.measurement: From front plane of structure (1st story) or floor level (2nd story)

roof_requirements._note: RS zone roof slope per §17.22.040. Much less restrictive than LA City's pitched-roof penalty.
roof_requirements.la_comparison: LA City penalizes flat roofs with lower height limit; Pasadena has no such penalty

floor_area_limits._note: RS zone upper-story area restriction per §17.22.050
floor_area_limits.impact: Limits 2nd story to half of 1st story footprint — forces wider, shorter buildings

encroachment_plane.impact: Building must fit below a plane starting 6 ft high at the side lot line, angled inward at 30° — constrains upper-story width on narrow lots

setbacks_ft._note: RS zone setbacks unconfirmed — need Table 2-3. RM values partially confirmed.

far._note: RS zones use context-sensitive FAR: max floor area = 135% of median house size within 500-ft radius. No fixed FAR number.

rent_stabilization.name: Measure H (Fair and Equitable Housing Charter Amendment)

ellis_act.special_circumstances: elderly 62+, disabled, families with children under 18

rent_stabilization.difference_from_la: LA RSO: pre-Oct 1978, 2+ units. Pasadena: pre-Feb 1995, 2+ units — covers far more properties

impact_fees.notes: RIF is notably higher than LA City linkage fee. New RIF ordinance adopted Jul 2025 — amounts may have changed.

sb1123.status: Planning Commission reviewed Aug 2025, directed staff to prepare zoning amendments
sb1123.current_rules: State defaults apply (ministerial approval, 1200 SF min parcel, up to 10 units)

unconfirmed: RS zone side/rear setbacks (need PMC Table 2-3)
RM lot coverage maximums (need PMC Table 2-4)
Updated RIF amounts from Jul 2025 ordinance
sources: Ordinance No. 7371 (Feb 22, 2021) — amends Table 2-3 RS/RM-12 height limits
Pasadena Measure H / Rental Housing Board

zone_mapping._note: San Marino is almost entirely R-1 single-family, split into 7 area districts by min lot size. No R-2/R-3/R-4. New RM1/MU zones added Nov 2024 (Ordinance O-24-1415) for Housing Element compliance.

height_ft._note: R-1 height varies by area district, not lot width (unlike Pasadena). SMMC §23.02.09.

height_notes.R1_all: 2-story max without CUP (§23.02.15). 3rd story requires discretionary approval — not by-right.
height_notes.R1_constraint_summary: 3-story effectively prohibited for SB 1123: 2-story limit is codified, 30-35 ft height cap, and 30° encroachment plane constrains upper floors. SB 1123 state preemption of story limit is untested.
height_notes.R1_measurement: Height measured from datum plane (avg of high/low existing grade on street-facing wall) to highest point including roof structures (§23.01.01)
height_notes.sb9: SB 9 units limited to 16 ft / 1 story / 8 ft 6 in plate height (§23.02.33)

plate_heights._note: No explicit plate height limits for standard R-1 construction — controlled by overall height cap + 2-story limit. DRC reviews plate height on case-by-case basis (§23.15.13).
plate_heights.sb9_note: SB 9 two-unit developments: 8 ft 6 in plate height max (§23.02.33)

roof_requirements._note: No minimum roof pitch or flat-roof penalty found in San Marino zoning code.

floor_area_limits._note: San Marino uses lot coverage + livable area percentages instead of FAR. No explicit 2nd-story area restriction like Pasadena's 50% rule.

lot_coverage_pct._note: Max lot coverage as % of actual lot area (SMMC §23.02.10). Also capped at statutory-lot % × statutory lot size, whichever is less.

livable_area_pct._note: Max livable area uses same percentages as lot coverage (§23.02.11). Effectively a FAR-like control.

floor_area_limits.large_lot_bonus: Coverage/livable area may increase by 10% of the difference between actual and statutory lot area
floor_area_limits.corner_lot_reduction: Districts V-VII: cannot exceed 90% of max without CUP

encroachment_plane._note: 30-degree structural encroachment line per §23.02.13. Identical to Pasadena's daylight plane.
encroachment_plane.exception: Garages/accessory buildings under 12 ft with no attached livable area are exempt
encroachment_plane.impact: Same as Pasadena — building must fit below plane starting 6 ft high at side lot line, angled inward at 30°

setbacks_ft._note: R-1 setbacks per SMMC §23.02.09. Very generous — much larger than Pasadena or LA.

lot_size_minimums_sf._note: Per SMMC §23.02.09. Most San Marino lots are well above 10K SF SB 1123 threshold.

far._note: San Marino does not use FAR. Building size controlled by lot coverage + livable area percentage system. See floor_area_limits.

rent_stabilization.notes: No local rent control. State AB 1482 only (5% + CPI cap, max 10%, on units 15+ years old).

impact_fees.notes: No city-level RIF found. School fee ($4.79/SF) is notably higher than Pasadena ($2.24/SF) and LA.

sb1123.status: No local SB 1123 ordinance adopted. City is resistant to densification — SB 9 ordinance includes 99-year affordability covenant.
sb1123.current_rules: State defaults apply (ministerial approval, 1200 SF min parcel, up to 10 units)

sb9_local_restrictions.note: These SB 9 restrictions should NOT apply to SB 1123 (different state law), but city may attempt to extend them

unconfirmed: R-1 plate height limits (may be DRC discretion only — no code standard found)
R-1 roof pitch requirements (none found in code)
R-1 2nd-story area restriction (none found — unlike Pasadena's 50% rule)
RM1 FAR and lot coverage (not explicitly stated in RM1 zone standards)
City-level development impact fees beyond school fee
Whether 99-year affordability covenant from SB 9 ordinance would be applied to SB 1123 projects
sources: Ordinance O-24-1415 (Nov 20, 2024) — Zoning Code Update adding RM1/MU zones
HCD Review of San Marino ADU Ordinance (Aug 26, 2024)

rent_stabilization.name: Tenant Relocation Assistance Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: No traditional rent control — just-cause eviction + relocation assistance for no-fault evictions. AB 1482 state cap applies.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Stabilization Ordinance (Ch. 11 BHMC)
rent_stabilization.notes: Covers apartments built before Feb 1995 with 2+ units. Annual increase set by Rent Stabilization Commission.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Stabilization Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: One of the strongest RSO programs in CA. Covers units built before Jul 1979 (Costa-Hawkins) but just-cause applies to all rentals.

rent_stabilization.name: Glendale Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Adopted 2019. Relocation assistance + just-cause eviction. Rent increase cap via AB 1482.

rent_stabilization.name: Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Just-cause eviction + relocation assistance. No traditional rent cap beyond AB 1482 state law.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Control Charter Amendment (Art. XVIII)
rent_stabilization.annual_increase_formula: Set by Rent Control Board (typically 60-75% of CPI)
rent_stabilization.notes: One of the oldest and strictest RSO programs in CA. Covers units with certificate of occupancy before Apr 10, 1979.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Control and Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Adopted 2022. Covers MF units built before Feb 1995. Includes just-cause eviction protections.

rent_stabilization.name: Rent Stabilization and Tenant Protection Ordinance
rent_stabilization.notes: Adopted 2019. Annual rent increase capped at 5%. Covers units built before Feb 1995.
No local rent control. State AB 1482 only.

rent_stabilization.name: LA County Rent Stabilization Ordinance (RSO)
rent_stabilization.notes: County RSO covers unincorporated areas. Units built before Feb 1995 with 2+ units. Annual increase set by CPI (max 3%).

sb1123.status: County defaults — state SB 1123 standards apply. Planning processed through LA County Dept of Regional Planning.
